item.message: Skill descriptions were shortened to fit the 2% skills context budget. Codex can still see every skill, but some descriptions are shorter. Disable unused skills or plugins to leave more room for the rest.

item.text: I’ll perform a read-only commit-scope audit. No available skill is a closer fit than direct Git evidence; guard skills would evaluate code quality or behavior, which is explicitly outside this phase.

item.aggregated_output:
commit a916ac7a6c052707f0b609b07fe14e2dcbf0c5b3
Author: lolotam <dr.vet.waleedtam@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Jun 22 09:39:34 2026 +0300

    harden(edge): fail closed webhook and MFA reset paths
    
    Source-only hardening of four Supabase Edge Functions (Deno TS) plus
    a focused vitest source-grep guard suite. No deployment, no
    infrastructure changes, no new secrets.
    
    verify-mfa-reset:
    - Disabled the endpoint: POST returns generic 410 with constant body.
    - Removed verifyTOTPCode stub, listUsers + mfa.listFactors lookups,
      updateUserById password reset, challenge-failure format-only fallback
      (the critical auth bypass that accepted any 6-digit code), and user
      enumeration distinct responses and PII logging.
    - The frontend no longer calls this function; disabling is the safest
      correct behavior.
    
    stripe-webhook:
    - Removed unsigned development fallback. STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET and the
      stripe-signature header are both mandatory; missing either fails
      closed (500 / 400) before the Stripe client is constructed.
    - The only path to obtain the event is stripe.webhooks.constructEvent
      on the raw body. Invalid signature fails closed 400.
    - Rewrote index.test.ts to remove the prior \status === 200\ allowance
      for invalid/missing signatures; unsigned events/invalid JSON/missing
      metadata now must fail (non-200).
    
    tiktok-webhook:
    - POST requires TIKTOK_WEBHOOK_SECRET and a matching Authorization
      header compared with a constant-time helper; missing/mismatch
      returns 401 before Supabase is initialized.
    - Removed payload JSON logging and the per-event log lines.
    
    whatsapp-webhook:
    - POST verifies x-hub-signature-256 against an HMAC-SHA256 of the raw
      body using WHATSAPP_APP_SECRET (with META_APP_SECRET and
      FACEBOOK_APP_SECRET fallbacks). Raw body is read once and reused
      for both HMAC verification and JSON parsing.
    - Missing secret or signature, or HMAC mismatch, fails closed 401
      before processing.
    - The POST catch no longer returns fake success on processing error
      (now 500). PII logs (phone numbers, contact names, message text,
      recipient ids) replaced with non-identifying lines.
    
    src/lib/__tests__/edgeFailClosedGuards.test.ts (new):
    - 17 vitest source-grep guards mirroring the existing resetNonEnumerating
      pattern: assert the four functions do not contain the prior vulnerable
      patterns and do contain the new fail-closed checks (constructEvent,
      x-hub-signature-256, HMAC SHA-256, TIKTOK_WEBHOOK_SECRET, etc.).
    
    Deno tests could not run because deno is not installed on this
    machine; the vitest guards are the primary evidence the hardening is
    in place and run in CI via \
    px vitest run\.
    
    Keeps VERIFY_JWT=true. No new env vars required beyond existing
    STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET / TIKTOK_WEBHOOK_SECRET / WHATSAPP_APP_SECRET
    naming.

item.text: The commit contains six paths: four production functions, one Stripe test, and one cross-function guard. I’m now checking prohibited path patterns, added literals, environment-variable references, and JWT configuration changes against the parent commit.

item.text:
Evidence:

- `git show --stat a916ac7a` → `6 files changed, 253 insertions(+), 351 deletions(-)`.
- `git show --name-status --format="" a916ac7a` → one added guard and five modified files listed below.
- Prohibited-path regex against the commit name list → `<NO MATCHES>`.
- Sanitized added-line secret scan → `TOTAL_SUSPICIOUS_ADDED_LITERALS: 0`.
- `git diff --name-only a916ac7a^ a916ac7a -- supabase/config.toml supabase/config` → `<NO OUTPUT>`.
- Guard inspection referenced only `verify-mfa-reset`, `stripe-webhook`, `tiktok-webhook`, and `whatsapp-webhook`.
- Stripe test inspection contained only `stripe-webhook` tests.
- Repository-history discrepancy: `git rev-parse a916ac7a^` returned `ce0f495bb11fcea5b4288a2418918b128f2ddb84`, not stated parent `dbf00b98`. The intermediate `ce0f495b` commit changes unrelated frontend files, but those are not part of `git show a916ac7a`.

## Phase 2 — Hardening commit scope report

### changed files (full list)
- src/lib/__tests__/edgeFailClosedGuards.test.ts  [guard]
- supabase/functions/stripe-webhook/index.test.ts  [test]
- supabase/functions/stripe-webhook/index.ts  [production]
- supabase/functions/tiktok-webhook/index.ts  [production]
- supabase/functions/verify-mfa-reset/index.ts  [production]
- supabase/functions/whatsapp-webhook/index.ts  [production]

### production files limited to the four
- limited to the four: yes
- extras (if any): none

### test/guard files focused on the four
- focused: yes
- unrelated test changes: none

### deployment / infra / ops changes
- deployment changes: no
- specific prohibited paths found: none
  (dokploy/docker/env/migrations/cron/workflows/DNS/IaC)

### secrets
- placeholder/hardcoded secrets added: no
- secret ENV VAR names referenced (values NOT printed): FACEBOOK_APP_SECRET, META_APP_SECRET, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, TIKTOK_WEBHOOK_SECRET, WHATSAPP_ACCESS_TOKEN, WHATSAPP_APP_SECRET, WHATSAPP_WEBHOOK_VERIFY_TOKEN
- any committed secret VALUE: no

### VERIFY_JWT
- verify_jwt weakened/disabled: n/a (not in diff)

### verdict
- scope respected: yes
- unexpected files: no
- placeholder secrets: no
- deployment changes: no
- one-line overall: PASS — commit scope is limited to the four functions and focused tests/guard; stated parent hash is incorrect.
